violations.finding: Contains Chinese text: `禁止 full revert` — verbatim user quote embedded in agent file body (not in docs/dev/ticket-*.md where it belongs per exemption rule)
violations.recommendation: Move verbatim user quote to docs/dev/ticket-*.md; use task-id attribution in the ba.md rule text; replace inline Chinese with English: 'revert is forbidden'

violations.finding: Contains Chinese trigger keywords: "又", "还是", "没修好", "第 N 次" listed as retry-detection strings in rule body. CJK literals must be replaced with English equivalents or removed.
violations.recommendation: Replace with English equivalents: 'again', 'still broken', 'not fixed', 'Nth time'; if verbatim CJK input-matching is genuinely required, document in docs/dev/ticket-*.md with task-id attribution and reference from ba.md

violations.finding: Contains multiple Chinese phrases in rule text: "超出用户实际需求范围的修改提议 — 必须仅入 ledger", "超出用户实际需求范围", "扩张式措辞" embedded directly in rule body of out_of_scope_observations chapter
violations.recommendation: Rule text must be English. Move verbatim Chinese phrases to docs/dev/ticket-*.md; replace with English paraphrases with task-id attribution

violations.finding: Contains extensive Chinese text throughout the Tone/Mission section: section headings with Chinese (画像, 引导, 本职, 完美, 整洁), inline Chinese in explanatory text ("最完美优先", "在此基础上实现最小实现", "github人人都会称赞", full Chinese verbatim user directive at line 543, "三件 mission", etc.)
violations.recommendation: Verbatim user directives in Chinese must be moved to docs/dev/ticket-*.md. Section headings and inline explanatory text must be English-only. Use task-id attribution to reference the source ticket.

violations.finding: Contains Chinese trigger keyword: "以前没问题" embedded in regression detection rule text
violations.recommendation: Translate to English: 'it used to work'; if verbatim CJK user-input matching is required, move to docs/dev/ticket-*.md with task-id attribution

violations.finding: Chinese characters "以及", "并且", "以前" used as detection keywords in rule text
violations.recommendation: Translate to English equivalents ('and', 'moreover', 'previously'); if needed for CJK detection, reference ticket with task-id attribution

violations.finding: Chinese character "修复" embedded inline in Codex instruction text: "out-of-path 修复 dressed as in-scope"
violations.recommendation: Translate to English: "out-of-path fix dressed as in-scope"

violations.finding: Chinese text "用户需求实测满足" embedded inline in technical field description
violations.recommendation: Translate to English: 'user requirement verified by live test'; reference original spec in English

violations.finding: Chinese text "dev 偏离 BA spec AC 但用户需求实测满足 = PASS，但 dev report 必须显式记录 AC 偏离原因" embedded inline in rule text as spec reference
violations.recommendation: Translate the rule text to English. If verbatim spec quote is needed, move to docs/dev/ticket-*.md with task-id attribution.

violations.finding: Chinese text "右侧有空白" embedded inline as example in verification rule — an English translation is already present next to it
violations.recommendation: Remove the Chinese example; the English translation already provided is sufficient: '(right-side gap)'

violations.finding: Contains Chinese Unicode range literal `[一-龟]` in detection bash code block used as an actual grep pattern (not a labeled example)
violations.recommendation: This is detection code embedded in an agent file. If kept, label clearly as a detection example. The Chinese range characters are functional not illustrative and violate english-only for agent files.

violations.finding: Contains Chinese text "核心清理目标" embedded in an example JSON block under Standard 6 detection
violations.recommendation: Replace with English placeholder: "Core Cleanup Targets" — the example already notes this as a violation example, but the actual Chinese string should be replaced

violations.finding: Inline bash code block with actual git commands (git log, git show, git log --reverse) that constitute implementation logic for Step 3 git root cause analysis, not labeled as examples
violations.recommendation: Extract to a script in scripts/ (e.g., scripts/git-root-cause.sh) with parameters; reference from ba.md as a script call

violations.finding: Inline bash code block with find/grep/ls commands against actual project paths for affected files discovery — implementation logic not an example
violations.recommendation: Extract to scripts/find-affected-files.sh with parameters; reference from ba.md as a script call

violations.finding: Inline bash code blocks invoking spec-check.py with actual --spec-id and --agent-id arguments — implementation logic for checkpoint marking
violations.recommendation: Extract checkpoint marking commands to a helper script; reference from ba.md

violations.finding: Inline bash code block in Step 3 for script execution — contains actual bash invocation logic
violations.recommendation: Extract to scripts/run-verification.sh; reference from qa.md

violations.finding: Inline bash code block with git diff commands in Step 4 verification — implementation logic
violations.recommendation: Extract to a verification script; reference from qa.md

violations.finding: Inline bash code block with python compile and npm build commands in Step 4 — implementation logic
violations.recommendation: Extract to scripts/build-verify.sh; reference from qa.md

violations.finding: Inline bash code block for reference integrity check in Step 4 — implementation logic
violations.recommendation: Extract to a validation script; reference from qa.md

violations.finding: Inline bash code blocks for code quality checks in Step 5 — multiple bash commands with actual logic
violations.recommendation: Extract to scripts/code-quality-check.sh; reference from qa.md

violations.finding: Inline bash code block for hardcode scanning in Step 5 — actual grep implementation logic
violations.recommendation: Extract to scripts/scan-hardcoded-values.sh; reference from qa.md

violations.finding: Inline Python test template code block — full Python implementation logic embedded in agent file
violations.recommendation: Extract to scripts/test-template.py; reference from qa.md as a template file

violations.finding: Inline python3 invocation with actual test execution logic — implementation logic not an example
violations.recommendation: Extract to a shell script wrapper; reference from qa.md

violations.finding: Inline bash permission check code block — implementation logic
violations.recommendation: Extract to a check script; reference from qa.md

violations.finding: Inline bash for loop — multi-line shell logic in agent file
violations.recommendation: Extract to a script; reference from qa.md

violations.finding: Inline bash code blocks for checkpoint marking (spec-check.py invocations) — implementation logic
violations.recommendation: Extract checkpoint marking commands to a helper script; reference from qa.md

violations.finding: Inline bash code block with python3 grep detection — implementation logic embedded in agent file body
violations.recommendation: Extract detection commands to scripts/detect-python3-calls.sh; reference from style-inspector.md

violations.finding: Inline bash code block for step numbering grep detection — implementation logic
violations.recommendation: Extract to scripts/detect-step-numbering.sh; reference from style-inspector.md

violations.finding: Inline bash find command for naming convention detection — implementation logic
violations.recommendation: Extract to scripts/detect-naming-violations.sh; reference from style-inspector.md

violations.finding: Inline bash grep for Chinese character detection — implementation logic
violations.recommendation: Extract to scripts/detect-chinese-chars.sh; reference from style-inspector.md

violations.finding: Inline bash grep block for marketing language detection — multiple grep commands forming implementation logic
violations.recommendation: Extract to scripts/detect-marketing-language.sh; reference from style-inspector.md

violations.finding: Direct python3 invocation without venv activation: `python3 tests/scripts/validate-<feature-name>.py --project-root .`
violations.recommendation: Add venv activation before python3 call: `source venv/bin/activate && python tests/scripts/validate-<feature-name>.py --project-root .`

violations.finding: Uses decimal step numbering: "Step 0.5" — non-integer step identifier
violations.recommendation: Resequence to integer: replace Step 0.5 with Step 1 and renumber subsequent steps

violations.finding: Uses decimal and lettered step numbering throughout: Steps 2a, 5a, 5a.2, 5b, 5c, 5c.0a, 5c.0, 5c.1, 5c.1.5, 5c.2, 5d — none of these are integers
violations.recommendation: Resequence all steps to integers: 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6... Eliminate all letter suffixes and decimal sub-steps

standards_passed: no-hardcoded-values (agents/INDEX.md and agents/README.md are auto-generated index files; style-inspector.md, ba.md, qa.md contain only example/schema values, not actual hardcoded script values)
meaningful-naming (no forbidden naming patterns: enhance-*, fast-*, optimize-*, *-v2, improved-*, better-*, new-* found)
merge-similar-scripts (not applicable to agent .md files — no scripts to merge)
concise-documentation (agent files are by design comprehensive procedural agents; content is functional specification not decorative verbosity)
parameterized-scripts (not applicable to agent .md files — no script parameters to check)
dangling-references (script references in qa.md and ba.md are example schemas and template names, not actual file references that must exist on disk)
merge-conflicts (no unresolved merge conflict markers found in any of the 5 files)

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex identified: (1) draft #8 (ba.md:545) is a duplicate of draft #4 (ba.md:523-563 range already covers line 545); (2) draft #2 recommendation incorrectly suggested USER_VERBATIM wrapping — CJK tokens must be replaced entirely not wrapped; (3) draft #13 location should be ba.md:746-753 not 744-750; (4) draft #14 was too broad — specific line ranges in qa.md should be enumerated; (5) missed violations in style-inspector.md (lines 352, 365 for Standard 6; lines 239-245, 272-276, 313-317, 350-353, 424-439 for Standard 1); (6) missed Standard 4 violations in ba.md:617 (Step 0.5) and qa.md (Steps 2a, 5a, 5a.2, etc.); (7) ba.md lines 1265-1280 constitute an additional Standard 1 violation; (8) qa.md lines 1818-1835 constitute an additional Standard 1 violation.

findings.issue: Draft #8 (ba.md:545) is duplicate of draft #4 — line 545 falls within the ba.md:523-563 range already covered
findings.proposed_fix: Remove draft #8 as a standalone violation; it is already captured under draft #4
findings.rationale: Line 545 is inside the 523-563 range. Keeping it as a separate entry would double-count the violation.

findings.issue: Draft #2 recommendation incorrectly suggested wrapping CJK tokens in USER_VERBATIM tags — the standard requires replacement not wrapping
findings.proposed_fix: Recommendation should read: 'Replace with English equivalents; if verbatim CJK user-input matching is genuinely required, document in ticket file with task-id attribution and reference from ba.md'
findings.rationale: Standard 6 requires English-only; the exemption is only for docs/dev/ticket-*.md files. USER_VERBATIM wrapping is not a recognized exemption mechanism.

findings.issue: Draft #13 location was ba.md:744-750 but should be ba.md:746-753
findings.rationale: Codex identified the correct line range from file read. Updated in final report.

findings.issue: Draft #14 (qa.md inline code) was too broad — specific line ranges must be enumerated for actionable findings
findings.rationale: Actionable findings require specific locations. The broad 'multiple step sections' citation is insufficiently precise for remediation.

findings.issue: Missed Standard 6 violations in style-inspector.md at lines 352 (Chinese range literal [一-龟]) and 365 (Chinese example 核心清理目标)
findings.proposed_fix: Add two Standard 6 findings for style-inspector.md:352 and style-inspector.md:365
findings.rationale: These are actual Chinese characters in an agent file, subject to Standard 6 regardless of their purpose as detection patterns or examples.

findings.issue: Missed Standard 1 violations in style-inspector.md — multiple bash detection blocks at lines 239-245, 272-276, 313-317, 350-353, 424-439 are inline executable code
findings.proposed_fix: Add Standard 1 findings for each of those line ranges in style-inspector.md
findings.rationale: These are bash blocks with actual detection logic (grep, find commands) embedded in the agent file body, not labeled examples.

findings.issue: Missed Standard 1 violations: ba.md:1265-1280 (cp-state spec-check.py bash blocks) and qa.md:1818-1835 (cp-state bash blocks)
findings.proposed_fix: Add Standard 1 findings for ba.md:1265-1280 and qa.md:1818-1835
findings.rationale: These are checkpoint marking bash blocks with actual spec-check.py invocations — inline implementation logic in agent files.

findings.issue: Missed Standard 4 violations: ba.md:617 uses 'Step 0.5' and qa.md uses Steps 2a, 5a, 5a.2, 5b, 5c, 5c.0a, 5c.0, 5c.1, 5c.1.5, 5c.2, 5d
findings.proposed_fix: Add Standard 4 findings for ba.md:617 and qa.md step numbering throughout
findings.rationale: Standard 4 requires integer-only step numbering. Decimal and lettered suffixes are clear violations.

findings.issue: Codex claimed ba.md lines 1250, 1266, 1275 are Standard 3 violations (bare python3 without venv)
findings.proposed_fix: PROPOSED_FIX: Mark as false positive — these lines use 'source venv/bin/activate && python3' which is the compliant pattern
findings.rationale: ba.md lines 1250/1266/1275 explicitly source the venv before invoking python3. This is the approved pattern per Standard 3. Not a violation.

codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: Applied 8 of 9 Codex findings: removed duplicate draft #8, corrected draft #2 recommendation, corrected draft #13 location, replaced broad draft #14 with specific line citations, added 2 missed Standard 6 violations in style-inspector.md, added 5 missed Standard 1 violations (style-inspector.md detection blocks + ba.md:1265-1280 + qa.md:1818-1835), added 2 missed Standard 4 violations. Rejected 1 finding: ba.md python3 lines are compliant (venv is sourced before python3 call).
